title: Angular Advanced — Paper 4
description: Advanced Angular concepts: reactive forms, custom validators, routing, guards, HTTP client, RxJS, change detection, custom directives/pipes, state management, and testing.

options.text: ['angular']
['rxjs']
['angular', 'rxjs']

questions.text: What does calling form.reset() do on a reactive form?

options.text: Destroys and recreates the FormGroup
Resets all control values to null (or a provided value), clears errors, and resets dirty/touched status
Only clears validation errors but keeps values
Removes all controls from the FormGroup

questions.text: In a reactive form, what does the emitEvent: false option do when calling setValue() or patchValue()?

options.text: Prevents the control from being marked as dirty
Prevents the valueChanges and statusChanges observables from emitting, avoiding triggering subscriptions during programmatic updates
Skips validation when the value is set
Prevents the form from being marked as touched

questions.text: Which of these correctly defines a ValidatorFn that ensures a control's value is a positive number?

options.text: export function positiveNumber(c: AbstractControl) { return c.value > 0; }
export function positiveNumber(c: AbstractControl): ValidationErrors | null { return c.value > 0 ? null : { notPositive: true }; }
export function positiveNumber(c: AbstractControl) { return c.value > 0 ? true : false; }
export function positiveNumber(c: AbstractControl) { return { notPositive: c.value <= 0 }; }

questions.text: In a custom async validator, what should you return if the server call fails and you want the control to be considered valid rather than in an error state?

options.text: throwError(() => new Error('Server error'))
catchError(() => of(null)) — returning null indicates no error
of({ serverError: true })
EMPTY
At the top of the routes array so it is checked first
At the bottom of the routes array so it only matches when no other route matches
In the forChild() module
Position does not matter — Angular always checks it last

questions.text: What is the relativeTo option used for in Router.navigate()?

options.text: To navigate relative to the root URL
To navigate relative to the current ActivatedRoute, resolving the path from the current route's position in the tree
To navigate to a parent route
To pass query parameters relative to the current URL

questions.text: A ResolveFn returns an observable. What happens if the observable throws an error during resolution?

options.text: Angular retries the resolve automatically
Navigation is cancelled and the router stays on the current route; the error can be caught in the effect or component
The component renders with null data
Angular redirects to the root route

questions.text: What is the role of the withComponentInputBinding() feature in Angular's provideRouter?

options.text: Enables two-way binding for router-link directives
Automatically binds route parameters, query params, and resolved data to @Input() properties of the routed component
Lazy loads component inputs from an API
Enables router animations between route changes
Cancels requests that take too long
Logs the request URL before sending and the response status code when the response arrives
Adds an authorization header to all requests
Retries failed requests and logs the retry count

questions.text: When using HttpClient with observe: 'response', what does the returned observable emit?

options.text: Only the response body typed as the generic parameter
The full HttpResponse<T> object including headers, status code, and body
A stream of HttpEvent objects as the upload/download progresses
The response as a raw text string
Immediately when subscribed
After both a$ and b$ have each emitted at least one value
After both a$ and b$ complete
After the faster observable (b$) completes

questions.text: What is a ReplaySubject(1) and how does it differ from a BehaviorSubject?

options.text: They are identical — ReplaySubject(1) is just an alias
ReplaySubject(1) replays the last 1 emission to new subscribers but has no initial value requirement; BehaviorSubject requires an initial value
BehaviorSubject replays all historical values; ReplaySubject only replays one
ReplaySubject cannot complete; BehaviorSubject can

questions.text: What does the async pipe do automatically when a component is destroyed?

options.text: Completes the observable
Unsubscribes from the observable to prevent memory leaks
Emits a final null value
Saves the last emitted value to local storage
undefined

questions.text: What does the @HostBinding('class.active') decorator binding do on a directive?

options.text: Adds a host listener for the 'active' event
Conditionally adds or removes the 'active' CSS class on the host element based on the bound property value
Creates a CSS variable named 'active'
Emits an event named 'class.active'
On every change detection cycle
Only when largeList or searchTerm references change (i.e., a new object/primitive value is assigned)
Only when the component is first initialized
Whenever an item is pushed to largeList

questions.text: In NgRx Entity, what is the EntityAdapter used for?

options.text: Connecting the store to a database entity
Providing pre-built reducers and selectors for managing collections of entities (CRUD operations) with normalized state
Generating HTTP requests for REST entity endpoints
Mapping API responses to TypeScript interfaces

questions.text: In Angular Signals, what does signal.update() do differently from signal.set()?

options.text: update() creates a new signal; set() modifies an existing one
update() takes a function that receives the current value and returns the new value; set() takes a direct new value
update() is asynchronous; set() is synchronous
They are identical in behavior
undefined
An observable that immediately emits [{ id: 1 }]
A promise resolving to [{ id: 1 }]
The array [{ id: 1 }] directly

questions.text: In an Angular component test, what is the purpose of NO_ERRORS_SCHEMA?

options.text: Prevents all template errors from being thrown during testing
Allows the compiler to ignore unknown elements and attributes in the template, useful for shallow testing without importing all child components
Disables runtime error handling in the test
Prevents Jasmine from catching errors in the component

questions.text: When using RouterTestingHarness in Angular tests, what does it provide over a plain TestBed setup?

options.text: It mocks all HTTP calls made during routing
It provides a testing environment with a real router and router outlet so you can navigate and test routed components with full routing lifecycle
It replaces the component template with a test-only template
It runs tests in a web worker

questions.text: What does the withInterceptors([myInterceptor]) function do in Angular's provideHttpClient?

options.text: Registers legacy class-based interceptors
Registers functional HttpInterceptorFn interceptors for the application
Provides a testing override for HTTP calls
Enables HTTP/2 for all outgoing requests

questions.text: What is the difference between ofType() in NgRx effects and filter() in RxJS when filtering actions?

options.text: ofType() is asynchronous; filter() is synchronous
ofType() filters by action type string AND narrows the TypeScript type of the action; filter() requires a manual type predicate for the same type narrowing
filter() can only be used inside effects; ofType() works anywhere
They are functionally identical

questions.text:
Given this template:
{{ item.name }}
 What does itemSize="50" represent?

options.text: The maximum number of items to render
The height in pixels of each item, required by the virtual scroll strategy to calculate which items are visible
The font size of each item
The number of items to buffer outside the viewport

questions.text: In Angular, what does the Renderer2.listen() method do?

options.text: Attaches an Angular event binding to the host element
Adds a DOM event listener in a platform-safe way and returns a cleanup function to remove it
Creates a custom RxJS observable from a DOM event
Subscribes to a BehaviorSubject inside a directive

questions.text: In Angular, what is the CSRF/XSRF protection mechanism HttpClientXsrfModule provides?

options.text: It encrypts all outgoing HTTP request bodies
It reads an XSRF token from a cookie and automatically attaches it to state-changing HTTP requests (POST/PUT/DELETE) as a header
It validates incoming responses for CSRF tokens
It blocks HTTP requests to cross-origin servers

questions.text: In Angular standalone components, how do you provide a service that should have a single instance for the whole application?

options.text: Declare it in providers: [] of every standalone component
Use providedIn: 'root' in the @Injectable decorator
Add it to bootstrapApplication's providers array
Both b and c are valid approaches

questions.text: What is the purpose of the FormControl's statusChanges observable?

options.text: Emits the current value whenever the control changes
Emits the control's validation status ('VALID', 'INVALID', 'PENDING', 'DISABLED') whenever it changes
Emits true when the control is touched
Emits the number of errors whenever validation runs
Removes duplicate click events based on event timestamp
Filters out consecutive emissions where the value (click event object) is the same reference, preventing duplicate processing
Limits the total number of emissions
Delays each emission by 300ms
